mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicate strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero during expansion phases, with Rank IC falling below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.005 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, accelerating inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 255.0, or the threshold effect fails to replicate in forward-looking tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor and housing market strength, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or housing starts decouple from payroll growth without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during periods of low interest rates.
failure_condition: Housing starts lose predictive power when rates exceed 3.0%, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of low-rate periods.
